1989_01_13-India-Mumbai-India_The_Land_Of_Dharma-OT-st2-vr01

Event Name/Title

India The Land of Dharma

Event Date

13 Jan 1989

Event Location

India

Event Type

Other

Transcriber

Nandrani Priya Devi Dasi

Completed

20 Sep 2021

Tags – Places

Click here to enter text.

Tags – Pastimes

Shringi rishi curses Maharaja Parikshit, Maharaja Parikshit give kali a place to stay

Tags – Persons

Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira, Sukhdeva Goswami, Maharaja Parikshit, Samika, Shringi, Kali, Brahmins

Tags – Themes

Service, humility, sacrifice, Brahminical culture

Lecture Transcription

In Bhagavad-gita Bhagavan Sri Krishna declares to us that a wise man is situated in self-realization. He considers a clot of dirt and gold to be the same. He considers honor and dishonor to be the same. He sees no difference between pleasures and pains inflicted upon the senses. In other words, such a person he is seeking his happiness within. Therefore, he rejoices within and he is illumined within. But one who is not intelligent enough to find the precious peace within…within his own heart, such a person like a beggar wander through various species of life in this world, seeking the enjoyment of the senses. We are living in the age which is called Kali Yuga. It is an age of quarrel and hypocrisy. It is an age where money is God. Where people are very anxious and hungry for the power to control. But very few people can appreciate the essence of life which is to become the humble servant.

It is described in the Srimad-Bhagavatam that the Pandavas after they were victorious in the battle of Kuruksetra, Lord Sri Krishna coronated Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira as the king. At that time this entire planet earth was one country. It was known as Bharat Varsha. And there was one supreme emperor and that was the emperor of the Kuru dynasty, who was Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira and there were many other kings in other lands, but each and every one of them they were obliged to offer respect and rule their kingdom according to the sweet will and direction of Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira.

So of course, the Pandavas were dear friends of Lord Sri Krishna. Personally, they had no interest in acquiring a kingdom because Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira was the very embodiment of dharma. Krishna out of his mercy towards all living beings he wanted to give everyone the chance to be given the proper direction in life. It is said in the third chapter of Gita,

yad yad ācarati śreṣṭhas
tat tad evetaro janaḥ
sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute
lokas tad anuvartate

[Bg. 3.21]

[Whatever action a great man performs, common men follow. And whatever standards he sets by exemplary acts, all the world pursues.]

What the great men do the common men follow. Whatever standard they set by their exemplary work; all the world pursued.

So therefore, we find within the scriptures there are many, many great mysteries of kings of the earth. In fact, in the twelfth canto of the Srimad-Bhagavatam there is a description by Sukadeva Goswami, what are the qualifications of an authorized purana and one of the most important characteristics of a genuine puranic literature is it must explain the history of the lives of great kings who were surrendered to the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord.

Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira in the mood of service

So we find that in Srimad-Bhagavatam there are a stories of King Parikshit, the story of Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira, the story of king Ambarisa, the story of King Dhurva, and King Prahalad,nd King Agnidra, and King Rshab Dev and King Bharat. We find the story of King Nimi, and King Janaka. These are a few of many descriptions. Because the king is supposed to be the representative of God in the kingdom. He is supposed to be Dharmaraj. He is supposed to be the protector of the principles of religion. The sanskrit word kshatriya mean one who protects the innocent from pain and the greatest pain is adharma or irreligion because where there is irreligion there will naturally be violence, thievery, exploitation and so many other vices.

Therefore, Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira he accepted the kingdom as a humble service of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. And it is described that after Krishna left the planet, He returned to His eternal abode Goloka Vrindavan and at that time Yudhiṣṭhira and his brothers had absolutely no more interest in remaining on this earth. How to understand their mentality? Most people to fight that battle in Kuruksetra it was such a tremendous effort to win a kingdom and he won the kingdom of the earth. He was the proprietor, actually the king is the proprietor of the entire land. One sixth of all wealth throughout the entire planet was liable to his taxation. He lived in the most luxurious palace. He had a loving wife, a beautiful family. He had power, control, money everything a man can desire beyond our imagination.

Here in Bombay, we have to struggle so hard to maintain one little flat and when thinking if we have a nice little flat a room for each member of the family and we get enough food, we are thinking how great we are. How much we are victorious in life. Imagine the living conditions of Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira.

Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira a Raja Rishi

But he was not the slightest bit attached to anything except the service to Krishna. He was accepting these things only on behalf of God. And as soon as Lord Shri Krishna left this world, Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira saw nothing attractive about his palace, nothing attractive about all of his hundreds and thousands of servants and maid servants and all of his wealth and all of his gold jewelry and his beautiful crown. Drtarashtra and Duryodhana, they were willing to beg borrow steal cheat do anything just to have that crown on their head. But Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira he didn’t see any difference between that crown and the lump of earth on the ground. He was only wearing it because it was a way that he could be the humble servant of Krishna and therefore after Lord Sri Krishna left his world Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira being responsible; please know that Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira was a sadhu.

Sadhu doesn’t mean someone who wears matted hair or shave his head and wears the saffron cloth. Sadhu according to the definition of Lord Kapila Deva is one whose every act, every word and every thought is in the service of Sri Hari, that is a Sadhu. In fact, such great kings were called upon by the name of Raja Rishi. Raja Rishi means Raja Rishi. Rishi mean sadhu, a saint, who happens to be the king. A saint is one who is the humble servant of God and therefore, Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira after he responsibly saw that Parikshit who was his grand-nephew was old enough and capable of ruling the kingdom with great joy he turned everything over to Maharaja Parikshit as soon as possible he just turned it all. He gave him his throne, he gave him his crown and all of his armies, all of his wealth, everything. Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira personally put on the cloth of mendicant and he took the staff of renunciation and with bare feet he began to walk to the jungles in the direction of the great Himalayas. Simply to show society that whoever you are you must maintain your responsibilities in this world in the service of God but when you come to that age where others can take over your responsibilities, your only business is self-realization. Your only business is to absorb your consciousness thoroughly in loving God, in the memory of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

Glories of Maharaja Parikshit

At that time Maharaja Parikshit following the footsteps of his grand-father Arjuna and his great predecessor Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira, he ruled the kingdom in the spirit of complete compassion. It is described that the king is responsible for each and every one of his what is called in sanskrit praja. Praja means the subjects. He did not only consider every human being without any discrimination of caste but he even considered the lower species as his praja. In other words, every citizen of his kingdom, even the plants and the animals and the women and the children and the rich and the poor and the literate and the illiterate they all deserved equally protection and they all deserve the right life in the service of God.

So, emperor Parikshit he would regularly personally mount on his great chariot which is drawn by great powerful black horses and he would travel throughout the world to make sure that each and every citizen would properly be guided in their spiritual life and protected from those who embodied irreligion. Now because he was so powerful and he was so strict in his discipline the whole world was very happy. The whole world was full of prosperity. Where does wealth come from? Prosperity comes from pleasing Krishna. Krishna says in Bhagavad-gita

yajṣārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra
loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ
tad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya
mukta-saṅgaḥ samācara

[Bg. 3.9]

[Work done as a sacrifice for Viṣṇu has to be performed; otherwise work causes bondage in this material world. Therefore, O son of Kuntī, perform your prescribed duties for His satisfaction, and in that way you will always remain free from bondage.]

When you perform proper sacrifice and please the lord then everything is provided for you. People in this world are thinking that rain comes from trees huh? Ecologist say that the more trees there are the more rain. This is a material conception. If we want to understand how this material world is working, we have to understand by the person who has created this world. Just as if you buy an IBM computer you don’t go to the local Indian oil petrol station to ask the mechanic how to work your computer. You have to find from the manufacturers of IBM how the proper computer is worked and how you can get the best function from it. So similarly, if we want to understand the laws of nature, we have to approach the law maker. If we want to understand how this creation is working, we have to hear from the manufacturer of this creation. In the Bhagavad-gita Krishna says,

mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ
sūyate sa-carācaram
hetunānena kaunteya
jagad viparivartate

[Bg. 9.10]

[This material nature, which is one of My energies, is working under My direction, O son of Kuntī, producing all moving and nonmoving beings. Under its rule this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again.]

That this material existence is working under My direction. It is created by Me. So, Krishna says in Gita

annād bhavanti bhūtāni
parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥ
yajṣād bhavati parjanyo
yajṣaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ

[Bg. 3.14]

[All living bodies subsist on food grains, which are produced from rains. Rains are produced by performance of yajṣa [sacrifice], and yajṣa is born of prescribed duties.]

This means that all living beings subsist on food grains and food grain is born from rain. Without rain there is no life, there is no food and he said rain is born from the performance of sacrifice. Sacrifices are born from the performance of one’s prescribed duties and prescribed duties all originate in the service of God. So, the ultimate sacrifice is the service of God when that is taking place it is described in Bhagavad-gita all the demigods are pleased. When you water the root of the tree, every part of the tree is satisfied. When Lord Hari is pleased by your service all the demigods who are in-charge of the various departments of this creation will provide plentifully everything and we read in the scriptures when there were great religious kings who were surrendering their lives in the humble service of the lord and who were the humble servant of every citizen when they were ruling then there was nothing but prosperity and joy throughout the land there was nothing in shortage. Now in the age we are living we may think it sounds like and eutopia. It sounds impossible. It sounds like mythology because of the very, very deep-rooted corruption all around us it does appear impossible but when there is true religion and pure leadership, it is natural.

Maharaja Parikshit meets Kali Personified

So therefore, Maharaja Parikshit upon his chariot he rode throughout the entire civilization making sure everyone was being properly protected and he was more wrathful than death personified for anyone who dared to exploit the innocent. At that time, he came across a very, very black hearted man. This person was Kali personified. He had a very, very dark complexion. He had very evil characteristics. He was low class in every respect. But on his head, there was crown of a king and on his body were the royal robes of a king. Then Maharaja Yudhiṣṭhira understood…Maharaja Parikshit understood that this is how Kali will conquer our lands. Through low class sinful minded people when they take the position of kings and leaders of nations, then there will not be the protection of the innocent, there will not be proper disciplinary actions upon the wicked. The laws of the state will not be one with the laws of God. There will be exploitation. In fact, it is prophesied that in the age of Kali Yuga the kings and the leaders who are supposed to protect the innocent will be the worst enemies of the innocent. It is described the taxation would be so cruelly enforced with such high rates that people will have to leave civilization and live in the wilderness and in the forests, in the jungle, just to escape the persecution of the leaders of nations of the government.

Characteristics of Kali personified

So, when Maharaja Parikshit came across this cruel, pretender in the form of a king, he saw that with a stick in his hand he was beating the innocent cow and beating the innocent bull. The cow and the bull were both standing with tears in their eyes. The cow is mother of humanity. She provides milk which is [00:21:18 – 00:21:20 inaudible] personified to everyone. She nurses practically all children in their infancy and the human species of life. She is the very embodiment of kindness and generosity and evolve like the father he is the personification of dharma or religion and his four legs represent the pillars of religion which are truthfulness, cleanliness, austerity and mercy. But here is a king is supposed to be very protector of these four pillars acting utterly merciless.

So, Maharaja Parikshit he need not depend on coexistence or legal devices empowered by God, he drew the sword and was about to kill this rascal and when Kali personified saw that he was in a hopeless condition standing before the representative of God, he did not have a chance. He got down on his hands and knees and he began to beg for mercy. I surrender to you. Any king will never kill a person who was surrendered. So, Maharaja Parikshit he said, “All right, because you have surrendered, it is my duty to release you.” He said, “You cannot disturb anyone as long as I am here. That is my command.” Kali told them that, “You are the king, you are providing for everyone. You must also provide a place for me.” Maharaja Parikshit said, “Yes, that is true. Therefore, I will give you four places to reside and in each of these four places you will be the king. You will have complete control wherever there is illicit sex, wherever there is any form of intoxication, wherever there is the slaughter of innocent animals and wherever there is whimsical gambling. You can be the king of all those places you can reside there and have complete control with all your wickedness.”

Kali with folded palms he responded, “Maharaja please understand that you had such a powerful king and none of these things exist in your kingdom. I have looked and searched throughout this entire planet and none of these things can take place because the robes and the thieves are so fearful of you, they have to act piously therefore no one is in fear and there was no sinful activities. So, what you have given me is all theoretical. You have to give me something practical. A place where I can go and reside.” The Maharaja Parikshit he said, “Yes what you are saying is true. Therefore, I will give you one more place. Wherever there is the greedy accumulation of wealth and gold you can live there because wherever people are hoarding wealth it was illicit sex, intoxication, gambling and meat eating you can follow them behind. When Kali was given that benediction, he was very satisfied.

Now Maharaja Parikshit as he ruled kali hardly found any place to live. How did kali yuga finally come into this world? Maharaja Parikshit did not let him in but understand that the illusory energy is very powerful and very strong. You see Maharaja Parikshit’s greatness was, he was always humble and obedient to the Brahmins, to the saintly persons. That is what makes a king great. As long as the King is the humble servant of the saintly brahmanas and that king is actually the representative of God but as soon as he takes the decisions of the kingdom in his own hands and in the hands of political ministers and he became the enemy of the people in general.

So therefore, the brahmin class was very powerful, and of course a brahmin is not by birth but by quality. Nowhere in Bhagavad-gita, nowhere in the Vedic scriptures is caste by the birth accepted. The Varnasrama system Krishna said in Gita is according to one’s qualities and with actions cātur-varṇyaṁ mayā sṛṣṭaṁ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśaḥ [Bg. 4.13].

Therefore, a brahmin is one who is peaceful, self-control, merciful, compassionate, forgiving, learned in the scripture and one who is dedicating his life in the service of God, that is the brahmana. There is nothing to do with wearing a fifty paisa thread around your body. Has nothing to do with what birth you are in. In fact, it is said by Lord Kapila Deva the author of the Sankhya philosophy, he describes that brahmānūcur nāma gṛṇanti ye te [Sb. 3.33.7].

That even if one who was born in the family of untouchable dog eaters, if he is chanting the name of God and living according to religious principles, he must be accepted as a Brahmana. Not only that, he is better than a brahmin. Because such a person it is described, he can deliver all humanity by his spiritual potency but someone with a so-called Brahmin who was not devoted in the service of God and calling out the names of God he cannot even deliver himself.

Description of how Kaliyuga was given a place

So, there was a Brahmana. It is a long story. He was the son of Samika Risi. His name was Shringi and he had the powers of a Brahmin but he did not have the purity of the heart and because of this Kali entered into this Brahmana. When the spiritual class of men become corrupt, the whole society crumbles and falls into utter degradation. So, through this Brahmin boy who is inexperienced by nature, he cursed Maharaja Parikshit to die in seven days and after he left and after the people of the world lost their trust in the Brahmins, due to this there was no longer a head in society. In Varnasrama system there is brahmin, kshatriya, vaishya and shudra. It is the parts of the social body. It is like you have a body. The head gives direction, the head gives intelligence, the arms protect, the belly nourishes and the legs carry the rest of the body. So similarly in the social system is like a body. The brahmins are meant to give direction and intelligence, the kshatriya’s are like the arms which protect, the vaishya’s they are like the belly which provides food and trade and the shudra’s they are the legs which serve and carry the rest of the parts of the body. Now, when the head is no longer qualified, then the whole body is lost. There is no direction.

So, when the Brahman class becomes corrupt what happens in the other classes of society lose their faith in their direction. Lose their faith in their wisdom and thus, nobody has any direction anymore. The religious principles are neglected. People do not understand what is their prescribed duties and what you have? You have capitalism and communism and socialism all these isms communism the shudra’s want the supreme control. Correct? Capitalism vaishya’s want supreme control and there are so many dictatorships where the ksatriya’s want supreme control. But factually the control of the society is meant to be in the hands of the Brahmin and the Brahmins are supposed to be completely in poverty. Factually in the Vedic civilization when it was true unless you were qualified to be a Brahmin you did not want to be a Brahmin. Because it was most difficult. A Brahmin was not allowed to own anything practically. He was not allowed to be employed. He had to go and beg arms like a humble bagger and whatever people gave him he had to give to the people in need. He didn’t keep anything for himself. He had to have complete control of his senses. Now there is not a single ksatriya, vaishya, shudra who wants to live like that. Therefore, when they saw someone with such genuine characteristics, they adored him they worshiped him, and they loved him, they followed him. When they saw such humility and such compassion and such mercy, they were willing to give their lives for such great souls. Krishna says,

namo brahmaṇya-devāya
go-brāhmaṇa-hitāya ca
jagad-dhitāya kṛṣṇāya
govindāya namo namaḥ

[Viṣṇu Purāṇa 1.19.65]

[Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is the prime protector of brahminical culture and the cow.] 

That Krishna is most favorably disposed to the Brahmins and the cows. Why? because when the Brahmins are properly respected and the cows are properly protected then the whole society is perfectly situated for material prosperity and spiritual enlightenment. But when there is no faith in the Brahmins and when the cows are being exploited, we can understand to that degree a society is rushing toward hellish conditions.

Our guru maharaja used to say, “That you can judge the spirituality of a nation or of a society very easily all you have to do is see how the cows are being taken care of. To the degree the cows are happy and joyful and protected, you can understand there is God consciousness and to the degree the cows are being misused, slaughtered and exploited you can understand there is irreligiosity and sinfulness pervading. Another criterion is how people have faith in saintly persons. When people lose their faith in saintly persons, they have no head anymore. When a society has lost faith in the Brahmanical culture that society is a headless society. It is a blind society. It has no vision; it has no eyes. When the blind lead the blind they all fall into the ditch.

So, you see when this inexperienced Brahmins curse the most righteous religious and God-fearing King, the people lost their faith in Brahmanical culture and then everyone started thinking and making their own decisions. We cannot trust these Brahmins, we cannot have trust these sadhus, we cannot trust these saints anymore. They are either in it for the money or either for the prestige or they are just a bunch of lazy people. How can I let my life be guided by such persons? Therefore, I will have my opinion and I will live according to that or I will accept the opinions of politicians and welfare workers but not the Brahmins. As soon as people lost their faith in saintly persons Kaliyuga had complete control of this whole world. We have just seen the condition of the world today. If you read the newspapers all you will see is corruption and violence based on greed, hunger for power, lust, anger, and utter illusion and there is only one solution to the problem. The only solution to the problem is we have to purify our consciousness and that purification of our consciousness can only take place when there is a restoration in heart of faith in saintly persons. Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has said,

‘sādhu-saṅga’, ‘sādhu-saṅga’ — sarva-śāstre kaya
lava-mātra sādhu-saṅge sarva-siddhi haya

[Cc. Madhya 22.54]

[“The verdict of all revealed scriptures is that by even a moment’s association with a pure devotee, one can attain all success.”]

That even one moment of association with a genuine sadhu can give one the opportunity for the supreme perfection of life. When Maharaja Parikshit was cursed to die in seven days what did he decide to do? To fall at the feet of the saintly persons and hear from them. He knew that, that was his only business. He knew that there was no greater gift of God than the association of sadhus and he left his body simply bathing in the nectarian words from Srila Sukadeva Goswami.

India – The Land of Dharma

So, this is a very very important part of our life and this is really the very glory of India. People do not come to India to solve their material ambitions; Indians goes to America to fulfil their material ambitions. I have been traveling between India and America for about twenty years now and I can say I have not met anybody from abroad is anxious to come to India for sense gratification. Ahh? But I can say in my travels through the world that I have met tens and thousands of Indians who go to the west for sense gratification. If you go to the American Embassy, you will find hundred’s, thousands of Indians who want to get somehow or other citizenship in America but I hardly ever found one American who wants to get citizenship in India. Why? Because Indians to a large extent are selling out the Brahmanical principles of spirituality they are simply chasing after like a madman the sensory objects of the West.

But when people want to come to India generally it is only for one person, I mean for one purpose, to find saintly people for their enlightment. Many people do come to India but they generally they are searching for something spiritual because India is the land of spirituality. One of the most important books of religion throughout the world is written by Chinese author. There was a book on world religion and what is religion. In the introduction it is said if you want to understand religion the only possible way this would have to go into India and live there for some time otherwise you will never really understand what is religion. It is the land of dharma. The glory of India is an its saintly persons but when saintly persons misuse their position and people lose their faith then they start putting their faith in the material facilities that are being offered by the sinful and degraded conditions of society and then everything is lost.
